claim: Alibaba cluster-trace-v2018 covers about 4000 machines over 8 days.
machine_usage.tar.gz is about 1.7 GiB compressed in the official release.
Springer Nature recommends its journal LaTeX authoring template and pdflatex-compatible local compilation.
Cluster Computing is the primary journal route for this workbench because the scope best matches cluster resource management.
The current workbench now contains a trained uncertainty-aware surrogate and a first aggregate-level proactive-control comparison.
The processed aggregate Alibaba v2018 workload artifact currently contains 11,208 observed one-minute samples.

The 2025 Cluster Computing workload-forecasting paper by Rossi et al. exposes a substantially larger evidence surface than the initial draft, with about 70 references and 11 figures visible on the official Springer article page.
The 2019 Cluster Computing autoscaling paper by Casalicchio exposes about 37 references and 7 figures on the official Springer article page.
The current predictive policy reduces over-provisioning from 0.3506 to 0.1410 relative to the reactive threshold baseline on the aggregate 5-minute evaluation route, while SLA violation is 0.0346.
Under the leakage-free split, linear regression achieves the lowest average MAE (3.514) across the 1-, 5-, and 10-minute horizons, while random forest is best at the 5-minute horizon (3.9283).
The current UA-MSTCN-Lite surrogate has empirical P90 coverage of 0.9199, 0.8665, and 0.8679 at the 1-, 5-, and 10-minute horizons, respectively.
A 60-minute context window gives the best average MAE (3.5476) for UA-MSTCN-Lite among the tested 30/60/120-minute settings.
Removing the predictive policy's scale-out margin raises SLA violation from 0.0346 to 0.1137, and removing cooldown raises scaling actions from 589 to 1617.
The high-coverage machine cohort contains 12 machines with coverage ratios between 0.9372 and 0.9472, and an average imputed ratio of 0.0581 after reindexing.
Across the 12-machine cohort, linear regression is the strongest average point baseline with MAE 3.6335, 5.5203, and 5.9659 at the 1-, 5-, and 10-minute horizons.
Across the 12-machine cohort, UA-MSTCN-Lite has average P90 coverage of 0.8909, 0.8527, and 0.8469 at the 1-, 5-, and 10-minute horizons.
In the 3-fold zero-shot transfer benchmark, pooled linear regression reaches MAE 3.6294, 5.5030, and 5.9528 on held-out machines at the 1-, 5-, and 10-minute horizons.
In the same zero-shot transfer benchmark, pooled linear regression matches the entity-specific linear upper bound within 0.02 MAE across all three horizons.
The global UA-MSTCN-Lite transfer model retains average P90 coverage of 0.9044, 0.8586, and 0.8529 on held-out machines.
The first service-level cohort extracted from official Alibaba container tables covers 2 app_du groups, 1003 containers, and a mean observed-minute coverage ratio of 0.9726.
Across the two-service cohort, UA-MSTCN-Lite achieves the lowest overall mean MAE (27.6253), ahead of random forest (28.1202), persistence (28.5998), and linear regression (29.4854).
Across the two-service cohort, UA-MSTCN-Lite retains average P90 coverage of 0.8906, 0.8831, and 0.8737 at the 1-, 5-, and 10-minute horizons.

Across the two-service cohort, predictive control reduces average over-provisioning from 21.4354 to 16.8214 while SLA violation rises from 0.0201 to 0.0234 and scaling actions rise from 140.0 to 386.5.
The service-level policy result is heterogeneous: predictive control improves app_141 but slightly worsens both SLA violation and over-provisioning on the burstier app_7876 service.
The expanded service-level cohort covers 10 app_du groups, 2036 selected containers, 1970 matched containers, and a mean observed-minute coverage ratio of 0.9713.
Across the expanded 10-service cohort, UA-MSTCN-Lite achieves the lowest overall mean MAE (7.4216), ahead of random forest (7.4752), linear regression (7.7623), and persistence (8.1760).
Across the expanded 10-service cohort, UA-MSTCN-Lite retains average P90 coverage of 0.8917, 0.8826, and 0.8660 at the 1-, 5-, and 10-minute horizons.

Across the expanded 10-service cohort, predictive control reduces average over-provisioning from 5.1767 to 4.4567 and average scaling actions from 481.2 to 428.8, while SLA violation rises from 0.0103 to 0.0434.

On the expanded 10-service cohort, predictive control improves over-provisioning for 4 of 10 services, reduces scaling actions for 7 of 10 services, and improves SLA violation for only 1 of 10 services.

The verified broad service cohort built from the full local container_usage mirror contains 139 app_du groups, 19,419 selected containers, and 19,269 matched containers over a common 11,520-minute span, with mean observed-minute coverage 0.9727 and mean container hit ratio 0.9932.
The top-10 overlap audit retains all 10 services from the earlier targeted cohort inside the new broad service cohort.
Across the promoted 139-service cohort, linear regression is the strongest average point forecaster with overall mean MAE 32.5705, ahead of persistence at 33.3813.
Across the promoted 139-service cohort, UA-MSTCN-Lite retains average P90 coverage of 0.9210, 0.8956, and 0.8766 at the 1-, 5-, and 10-minute horizons.

Across the promoted 139-service cohort, predictive control raises mean SLA violation from 0.0173 to 0.0311 and mean scaling actions from 249.0 to 399.4 relative to reactive control, while mean over-provisioning falls from 56.3105 to 53.6774.
Paired bootstrap over services shows that, on the promoted broad cohort, the equal-weighted predictive-minus-reactive SLA delta is +0.0138 with a 95% interval [0.0035, 0.0271], a stable degradation.

Paired bootstrap over services shows that, on the promoted broad cohort, the equal-weighted predictive-minus-reactive scaling-actions delta is +150.4460 with a 95% interval [114.0495, 184.9933], while the mean over-provisioning delta remains negative at -2.6331 but its interval [-6.9527, 2.3283] crosses zero.

The promotion audit requires the broad cohort to replace the earlier top-10 service mainline because the forecasting headline flips, the service-level scaling-actions sign flips, and the apparent over-provisioning gain is no longer stably supportable.

On the promoted broad cohort, predictive control improves SLA violation for only 27 of 139 services and reduces scaling actions for only 32 of 139 services, although it reduces over-provisioning for 95 of 139 services.
